block_00: How to Enter the Forex Market
block_01: As the world’s largest and most liquid market, Forex can offer traders many opportunities — as long as you know where to look, how to approach trading, and be aware of the risks involved.
block_02: As always, we have your back.
block_03: Here’s everything you need to know about Forex trading.
block_05: In the Forex market, national currencies are traded in pairs. Essentially, it is simultaneously buying one country’s currency and selling another country’s currency.
block_14: In a currency pair, the first currency is the base currency. It serves as the basis for the buy and sell of the trade.
block_15: To illustrate, let’s look at the
block_21: If you think EUR is going to perform better than USD, you would BUY a EUR/USD order.
block_23: If you think EUR will depreciate against USD, you would SELL a EUR/USD order.
block_24: Now, let’s say for example that the British economy is slowing down. You notice that, in comparison, the American economy seems to be gaining momentum.
block_25: Which of the following orders would you execute?
block_32: If you said B) Sell GBP/USD, you are correct! Since you anticipate GBP to depreciate against USD, you would sell GBP/USD.
block_34: There are many tools that may be used to manage risk and enhance your trading skills.
block_35: You may use a multiplier to increase your investment amount.*
block_36: Set up stop-loss/take-profit to close positions when a certain price level is reached
